mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies cluster management on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free Kubernetes experience with built-in integrations for Linode services. Key trade-offs include reliance on Linode's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -sL https://github.com/linode/linode-cli/releases/download/v2.0.0/linode-cli-2.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This installs the Linode CLI binary.
> Make the CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version and node count.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config

```

> This command retrieves the kubeconfig and saves it to your local kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster to enhance its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> This applies the necessary configurations for the Linode Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Ensure you have backups of any important data before proceeding.

uninstall.title: Remove the Linode CLI
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Linode CLI if it is no longer needed.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This command removes the Linode CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups and drain the nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Re-enable scheduling on the drained node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not fully initialized or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and try again. Check the cluster status with 'linode-cli k8s cluster-view my-k8s-cluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig file and ensure the cluster is running with 'linode-cli k8s cluster-view my-k8s-cluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is not properly configured or is experiencing issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs with 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available capacity in your Linode account.

**Fix:**
Adjust the node count or size in your cluster configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new scheduling features and improved resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs related to node management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure to review the new scheduling configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, indicating that your cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
